title: Branches, Integration, and Merge Evidence · Repository Trace: Post Merge Verification
summary: Integrate a short-lived change while preserving understandable topology and verifying both file content and combined behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git post merge verification increment in a branch-and-merge topology lab. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Verify merged tree, combined tests, log topology, diff range, and push target before publishing integration.
Distinguish fast-forward, true merge, squash, and no-commit integration by topology and team intent.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Post Merge Verification
steps.instruction:
theory case — Branches main and repair diverge from merge base b00b1e5. repair changes validation while main changes output formatting; predict fast-forward possibility, resulting parents, and post-merge behavior evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Post Merge Verification contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for verify merged tree, combined tests, log topology, diff range, and push target before publishing integration.

steps.title: Bound Post Merge Verification
steps.instruction:
theory case — Branches main and repair diverge from merge base b00b1e5. repair changes validation while main changes output formatting; predict fast-forward possibility, resulting parents, and post-merge behavior evidence.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Post Merge Verification application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for verify merged tree, combined tests, log topology, diff range, and push target before publishing integration.

content.text: Verify merged tree, combined tests, log topology, diff range, and push target before publishing integration. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Branches main and repair diverge from merge base b00b1e5. repair changes validation while main changes output formatting; predict fast-forward possibility, resulting parents, and post-merge behavior evidence. A defensible solution applies Post Merge Verification, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a branch-and-merge topology lab.

content.code:
base=$(git merge-base main repair)
printf 'base=%s\n' "$base"
git diff "$base"..repair
git ls-files --unmerged
# Resolve intent, stage, test, then continue.
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: No conflict markers means the merged behavior is correct. This fails the Post Merge Verification contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Post Merge Verification evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-post-merge-verification-10zq4s" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Post Merge Verification into observable performance evidence.

steps.title: Defend the Post Merge Verification model
